message:
fix(dolt): idle-monitor single-instance lock, port config isolation, project UUID verification (GH#2367, GH#2372)

Three P1 fixes for the Dolt server stabilization epic (bd-109c):

1. Idle-monitor single-instance enforcement (GH#2367): RunIdleMonitor now
   acquires an exclusive file lock at startup. If another monitor is already
   running, the new one exits immediately. This prevents the accumulation
   bug where Start() called from within the monitor watchdog would fork
   additional monitors (one per minute, ~32MB each).

2. Port config isolation (GH#2372): The port file (dolt-server.port, now
   gitignored) is elevated to primary source for port resolution, ahead of
   metadata.json and config.yaml which are git-tracked and can propagate
   to collaborators. metadata.json DoltServerPort emits a deprecation
   warning when used.

3. Client-side project identity verification (GH#2372): bd init now generates
   a UUID stored in both metadata.json (project_id) and the database metadata
   table (_project_id). On every store open, these are compared. A mismatch
   means the client is connected to the wrong projects Dolt server and the
   connection is refused with a clear diagnostic message.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
